stem: A company pays its employees a base salary of $200 per week plus $15 for each unit produced. An employee wants to earn at least $500 in a week. The employee can produce at most 25 units per week. What is the least number of units the employee must produce to meet the earning goal?
explanation: Set up the inequality: $200 + 15u \geq 500$. Subtract 200: $15u \geq 300$. Divide by 15: $u \geq 20$. The employee must produce at least 20 units. Since $20 \leq 25$, this is achievable.

distractorRationale.A: Cluster B error: Subtracted the base salary incorrectly as $500 - 200 = 300$, then divided $300 \div 15 = 20$, but subtracted 2 thinking the base salary covers the first 2 units, getting 18.
distractorRationale.C: Cluster C error: Interpreted 'at least $500' as 'more than $500,' solved $200 + 15u > 500$, got $u > 20$, and chose 21.
distractorRationale.D: Cluster D error: Ignored the base salary entirely and solved $15u \geq 500$, getting $u \geq 33.3$, rounded up to 34, ignoring the 25-unit capacity constraint.
